title: Salary + Let-Out House Property
description: Taxpayer has salary income and rental income from a let-out property. Rent is taxable under 'Income from House Property' after a standard deduction of 30% and municipal taxes paid. Home loan interest (u/s 24b) is fully deductible from let-out HP income (no ₹2L cap that applies to SOP). Can file ITR-1 if total income ≤ ₹50L and only one HP.

prevalence.description: Urban property owners who have moved cities for work and let out their original property.

typical_evidence.notes: AIS may show rent received (SFT-002 for rent > ₹50,000 per transaction). Cross-check with actual rent received.
26AS for TDS on rent if tenant deducted TDS u/s 194I (rent > ₹50,000/month from a business tenant) or u/s 194IB (rent > ₹50,000/month from individual tenant).

applicable_decisions.notes: Let-out HP income does not by itself disqualify ITR-1. ITR-1 can accommodate one HP (SOP or let-out). If TWO properties exist, ITR-2 is needed.
Old Regime allows full deduction of home loan interest on let-out property (no cap). New Regime restricts this. If home loan interest is substantial, Old Regime may be better.
TDS on rent may be present. Verify in 26AS Part A2 (u/s 194IB — individual tenant) or Part A (u/s 194I — business tenant).
Tax due or refund depends on net rental income and home loan interest deduction.

common_validations.notes: AIS may show rent received (if tenant filed Form 26QC). Cross-check actual rent with AIS.
Outcome varies — home loan interest may create a net loss from HP, which reduces overall tax.

intake_questions.question: How many properties do you own?
intake_questions.purpose: Two or more properties → ITR-2 required (cannot use ITR-1).

intake_questions.question: What was the total annual rent received from all let-out properties in FY 2024-25?
intake_questions.purpose: Compute Gross Annual Value (GAV) for HP income computation.

intake_questions.question: Did you pay municipal taxes (property tax) on the let-out property during FY 2024-25?
intake_questions.purpose: Municipal taxes paid are deductible from GAV to arrive at Net Annual Value (NAV).

intake_questions.question: Do you have a home loan on the let-out property? If yes, what was the interest paid during FY 2024-25?
intake_questions.purpose: Home loan interest on let-out property is fully deductible u/s 24b (no cap). Creates a large deduction in Old Regime.

edge_cases.condition: Two or more properties (even if one is SOP and one is let-out)
edge_cases.consequence: One of the properties is deemed let-out and rental income must be imputed. ITR-1 can handle only ONE HP. Must use ITR-2.

edge_cases.condition: Net loss from house property due to large home loan interest
edge_cases.consequence: Loss from HP can be set off against salary income up to ₹2L per year in Old Regime (New Regime: no setoff). Carry forward of balance loss.

notes: New Regime does not allow deduction of home loan interest on let-out property (unlike the Old Regime where it is unlimited). This is a significant factor in regime selection for this pattern.
